frameworks.name: valence-encoding architecture
frameworks.one_line: Parallel positive/negative valence channels encode aesthetic affect in distinct regions.

core_question: Which neural structures encode the aesthetic pleasantness versus unpleasantness of harmonious versus disharmonious color pairings?
key_assumptions: Color harmony judgments reflect a graded aesthetic-affective experience
Positive and negative aesthetic valence may be encoded by separable substrates
manipulations: Harmony level of paired-color stimuli (harmonious vs disharmonious combinations)
Hue, chroma, and lightness differences between paired colors
key_findings: Medial orbitofrontal cortex activity scaled positively with perceived color harmony
Amygdala activity scaled with perceived disharmony
interpretations: A parallel valence-encoding architecture underlies aesthetic experience of color
Aesthetic and emotional aspects of color perception map onto reward- and threat-related circuits
